title: Turbulent seas for the VLCCs
summary:
Main
By Ioannis Papadimitriou

In this insight, Vortexa examines the current status-quo for VLCC rates, and provides an outlook of whether the repositioning to the Western hemisphere can prove to be supportive in the months to come. After a short-lived spike during the start of April, VLCC rates have tumbled, losing around 25% of the value on the most heavily traded route worldwide: Middle East-to-East Asia (TD3C). The main drivers for this shortfall lie on both the export and the importing side:
OPEC crude exports are set to drop for the third consecutive month in May.
